steps.action: Zaouli or Zawli is a traditional dance of the Gu with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, hold hands, partner opposite, accent beat, grounded.
steps.text: Zaouli or Zawli is a traditional dance of the Gu with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, hold hands, partner opposite, accent beat, grounded.

steps.action: It is performed by a single masked and costumed
steps.text: It is performed by a single masked and costumed dancer, accompanied by musicians.

steps.action: Zaouli is also one of the most difficult dances
steps.text: Zaouli is also one of the most difficult dances in the world due its unique and difficult steps.

steps.action: The dance is performed by a single male dancer w
steps.text: The dance is performed by a single male dancer wearing a mask depicting a girl.

steps.action: Each Guro village has a local Zaouli dancer (alw
steps.text: Each Guro village has a local Zaouli dancer (always male), performing during funerals and celebrations.

steps.action: The dance is believed to increase the productivi
steps.text: The dance is believed to increase the productivity of the village that it is performed in and is seen as a tool of unity for the Guro community, and by extension the whole country.

steps.action: Zaouli rose in prominence within the Ivory Coast
steps.text: Zaouli rose in prominence within the Ivory Coast because it was the favorite dance of Ivorian president Félix Houphouet-Boigny and was regularly broadcast on national TV as a result.

steps.action: included a clip of a Zaouli dancer in her music
steps.text: included a clip of a Zaouli dancer in her music video for the song "Warriors", released as part of the video Matahdatah Scroll 01: Broader Than A Border in 2015.

steps.action: In 2022, the K-pop girl group Nature released a
steps.text: In 2022, the K-pop girl group Nature released a music video for their single "Rica Rica", featuring choreography which is heavily inspired by Zaouli dance.

steps.action: Short videos of traditional Zaouli dances are al
steps.text: Short videos of traditional Zaouli dances are also circulating on the Internet, the soundtrack of which has been replaced by fast, seemingly appropriate rhythms of psytrance music.

steps.action: A Zaouli dancer appears in a crowd of people as
steps.text: A Zaouli dancer appears in a crowd of people as part of a surreal musical sequence in the 2025 film Sinners.

steps.action: Claudie Haxaire traces its origin to the village
steps.text: Claudie Haxaire traces its origin to the village of Zougounéfla.

steps.action: However, stories on the origins of the character
steps.text: However, stories on the origins of the characteristic mask are varied, and each mask may have its own symbolic history.

steps.action: It was inscribed in 2017 on the Representative L
steps.text: It was inscribed in 2017 on the Representative List of the Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity by UNESCO.

steps.action: Some internet users have criticised this use as
steps.text: Some internet users have criticised this use as inappropriate.
